Package [org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime](<package-summary.html>)

# Interface WatermarkStrategy<T>

All Superinterfaces:
    `[Serializable](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/io/Serializable.html> "class or interface in java.io")`, `[TimestampAssignerSupplier](<TimestampAssignerSupplier.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<T>`, `[WatermarkGeneratorSupplier](<WatermarkGeneratorSupplier.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<T>`

All Known Subinterfaces:
    `[WatermarkStrategyWithPeriodicWatermarks](<../../../streaming/runtime/operators/util/WatermarkStrategyWithPeriodicWatermarks.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.util")<T>`, `[WatermarkStrategyWithPunctuatedWatermarks](<../../../streaming/runtime/operators/util/WatermarkStrategyWithPunctuatedWatermarks.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.util")<T>`

All Known Implementing Classes:
    `[AscendingTimestampExtractor](<../../../streaming/api/functions/timestamps/AscendingTimestampExtractor.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.timestamps")`, `[BoundedOutOfOrdernessTimestampExtractor](<../../../streaming/api/functions/timestamps/BoundedOutOfOrdernessTimestampExtractor.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.timestamps")`, `[PeriodicWatermarkAssignerWrapper](<../../../table/runtime/operators/wmassigners/PeriodicWatermarkAssignerWrapper.html> "class in org.apache.flink.table.runtime.operators.wmassigners")`, `[PunctuatedWatermarkAssignerWrapper](<../../../table/runtime/operators/wmassigners/PunctuatedWatermarkAssignerWrapper.html> "class in org.apache.flink.table.runtime.operators.wmassigners")`

* * *

[@Public](<../../../annotation/Public.html> "annotation interface in org.apache.flink.annotation") public interface WatermarkStrategy<T> extends [TimestampAssignerSupplier](<TimestampAssignerSupplier.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<T>, [WatermarkGeneratorSupplier](<WatermarkGeneratorSupplier.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<T>

The WatermarkStrategy defines how to generate [`Watermark`](<Watermark.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")s in the stream sources. The WatermarkStrategy is a builder/factory for the [`WatermarkGenerator`](<WatermarkGenerator.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime") that generates the watermarks and the [`TimestampAssigner`](<TimestampAssigner.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime") which assigns the internal timestamp of a record.

This interface is split into three parts: 1) methods that an implementor of this interface needs to implement, 2) builder methods for building a `WatermarkStrategy` on a base strategy, 3) convenience methods for constructing a `WatermarkStrategy` for common built-in strategies or based on a [`WatermarkGeneratorSupplier`](<WatermarkGeneratorSupplier.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")

Implementors of this interface need only implement `createWatermarkGenerator(WatermarkGeneratorSupplier.Context)`. Optionally, you can implement `createTimestampAssigner(TimestampAssignerSupplier.Context)`.

The builder methods, like `withIdleness(Duration)` or `createTimestampAssigner(TimestampAssignerSupplier.Context)` create a new ` WatermarkStrategy` that wraps and enriches a base strategy. The strategy on which the method is called is the base strategy.

The convenience methods, for example `forBoundedOutOfOrderness(Duration)`, create a `WatermarkStrategy` for common built in strategies.

This interface is [`Serializable`](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/io/Serializable.html> "class or interface in java.io") because watermark strategies may be shipped to workers during distributed execution.

    * ### createTimestampAssigner

default [TimestampAssigner](<TimestampAssigner.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<[T](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "type parameter in WatermarkStrategy")> createTimestampAssigner([TimestampAssignerSupplier.Context](<TimestampAssignerSupplier.Context.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime") context)

Instantiates a [`TimestampAssigner`](<TimestampAssigner.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime") for assigning timestamps according to this strategy.

Specified by:
    `[createTimestampAssigner](<TimestampAssignerSupplier.html#createTimestampAssigner\(org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime.TimestampAssignerSupplier.Context\)>)` in interface `[TimestampAssignerSupplier](<TimestampAssignerSupplier.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<[T](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "type parameter in WatermarkStrategy")>`
    * ### getAlignmentParameters

[@PublicEvolving](<../../../annotation/PublicEvolving.html> "annotation interface in org.apache.flink.annotation") default [WatermarkAlignmentParams](<WatermarkAlignmentParams.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime") getAlignmentParameters()

Provides configuration for watermark alignment of a maximum watermark of multiple sources/tasks/partitions in the same watermark group. The group may contain completely independent sources (e.g. File and Kafka).

Once configured Flink will "pause" consuming from a source/task/partition that is ahead of the emitted watermark in the group by more than the maxAllowedWatermarkDrift.

    * ### withTimestampAssigner

default [WatermarkStrategy](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<[T](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "type parameter in WatermarkStrategy")> withTimestampAssigner([TimestampAssignerSupplier](<TimestampAssignerSupplier.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<[T](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "type parameter in WatermarkStrategy")> timestampAssigner)

Creates a new `WatermarkStrategy` that wraps this strategy but instead uses the given [`TimestampAssigner`](<TimestampAssigner.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime") (via a [`TimestampAssignerSupplier`](<TimestampAssignerSupplier.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")).

You can use this when a [`TimestampAssigner`](<TimestampAssigner.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime") needs additional context, for example access to the metrics system.

          WatermarkStrategy<Object> wmStrategy = WatermarkStrategy
             .forMonotonousTimestamps()
             .withTimestampAssigner((ctx) -> new MetricsReportingAssigner(ctx));


    * ### withTimestampAssigner

default [WatermarkStrategy](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<[T](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "type parameter in WatermarkStrategy")> withTimestampAssigner([SerializableTimestampAssigner](<SerializableTimestampAssigner.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<[T](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "type parameter in WatermarkStrategy")> timestampAssigner)

Creates a new `WatermarkStrategy` that wraps this strategy but instead uses the given [`SerializableTimestampAssigner`](<SerializableTimestampAssigner.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime").

You can use this in case you want to specify a [`TimestampAssigner`](<TimestampAssigner.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime") via a lambda function.

          WatermarkStrategy<CustomObject> wmStrategy = WatermarkStrategy
             .<CustomObject>forMonotonousTimestamps()
             .withTimestampAssigner((event, timestamp) -> event.getTimestamp());


    * ### withIdleness

default [WatermarkStrategy](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<[T](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "type parameter in WatermarkStrategy")> withIdleness([Duration](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/time/Duration.html> "class or interface in java.time") idleTimeout)

Creates a new enriched [`WatermarkStrategy`](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime") that also does idleness detection in the created [`WatermarkGenerator`](<WatermarkGenerator.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime").

Add an idle timeout to the watermark strategy. If no records flow in a partition of a stream for that amount of time, then that partition is considered "idle" and will not hold back the progress of watermarks in downstream operators.

Idleness can be important if some partitions have little data and might not have events during some periods. Without idleness, these streams can stall the overall event time progress of the application.

    * ### withWatermarkAlignment

[@PublicEvolving](<../../../annotation/PublicEvolving.html> "annotation interface in org.apache.flink.annotation") default [WatermarkStrategy](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<[T](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "type parameter in WatermarkStrategy")> withWatermarkAlignment([String](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html> "class or interface in java.lang") watermarkGroup, [Duration](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/time/Duration.html> "class or interface in java.time") maxAllowedWatermarkDrift)

Creates a new [`WatermarkStrategy`](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime") that configures the maximum watermark drift from other sources/tasks/partitions in the same watermark group. The group may contain completely independent sources (e.g. File and Kafka).

Once configured Flink will "pause" consuming from a source/task/partition that is ahead of the emitted watermark in the group by more than the maxAllowedWatermarkDrift.

Parameters:
    `watermarkGroup` \- A group of sources to align watermarks
    `maxAllowedWatermarkDrift` \- Maximal drift, before we pause consuming from the source/task/partition
    * ### withWatermarkAlignment

[@PublicEvolving](<../../../annotation/PublicEvolving.html> "annotation interface in org.apache.flink.annotation") default [WatermarkStrategy](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<[T](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "type parameter in WatermarkStrategy")> withWatermarkAlignment([String](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html> "class or interface in java.lang") watermarkGroup, [Duration](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/time/Duration.html> "class or interface in java.time") maxAllowedWatermarkDrift, [Duration](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/time/Duration.html> "class or interface in java.time") updateInterval)

Creates a new [`WatermarkStrategy`](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime") that configures the maximum watermark drift from other sources/tasks/partitions in the same watermark group. The group may contain completely independent sources (e.g. File and Kafka).

Once configured Flink will "pause" consuming from a source/task/partition that is ahead of the emitted watermark in the group by more than the maxAllowedWatermarkDrift.

Parameters:
    `watermarkGroup` \- A group of sources to align watermarks
    `maxAllowedWatermarkDrift` \- Maximal drift, before we pause consuming from the source/task/partition
    `updateInterval` \- How often tasks should notify coordinator about the current watermark and how often the coordinator should announce the maximal aligned watermark.
    * ### forMonotonousTimestamps

static <T> [WatermarkStrategy](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<T> forMonotonousTimestamps()

Creates a watermark strategy for situations with monotonously ascending timestamps.

The watermarks are generated periodically and tightly follow the latest timestamp in the data. The delay introduced by this strategy is mainly the periodic interval in which the watermarks are generated.

See Also:

      * [`AscendingTimestampsWatermarks`](<AscendingTimestampsWatermarks.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")
    * ### forBoundedOutOfOrderness

static <T> [WatermarkStrategy](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<T> forBoundedOutOfOrderness([Duration](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/time/Duration.html> "class or interface in java.time") maxOutOfOrderness)

Creates a watermark strategy for situations where records are out of order, but you can place an upper bound on how far the events are out of order. An out-of-order bound B means that once the an event with timestamp T was encountered, no events older than `T - B` will follow any more.

The watermarks are generated periodically. The delay introduced by this watermark strategy is the periodic interval length, plus the out of orderness bound.

See Also:

      * [`BoundedOutOfOrdernessWatermarks`](<BoundedOutOfOrdernessWatermarks.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")
    * ### forGenerator

static <T> [WatermarkStrategy](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<T> forGenerator([WatermarkGeneratorSupplier](<WatermarkGeneratorSupplier.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<T> generatorSupplier)

Creates a watermark strategy based on an existing [`WatermarkGeneratorSupplier`](<WatermarkGeneratorSupplier.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime").

    * ### noWatermarks

static <T> [WatermarkStrategy](<WatermarkStrategy.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.eventtime")<T> noWatermarks()

Creates a watermark strategy that generates no watermarks at all. This may be useful in scenarios that do pure processing-time based stream processing.
